title: TypeScript with Code Snippets Quiz
description: A 10-question quiz about TypeScript, object orientation, interfaces, instance variables, and polymorphism. Each question references a TypeScript code snippet.

options.text: A private variable named `Animal`
A structure requiring a `name` property and a `speak` method
A runtime object that is automatically created
A class with implemented behavior
Because `Dog` is an interface
Because every TypeScript class always has `move()`
Because `Dog` extends `Animal`
Because `bark()` automatically creates `move()`
The method `fly()` is optional
The class becomes private
The class must provide the methods required by the interface
The interface inherits from the class
Polymorphism
Encapsulation
Casting
Overloading of variables
Because `Person` is a built-in JavaScript class
Because `user` matches the structure required by `Person`
Because TypeScript ignores interface definitions
Because interfaces only check methods, never properties
Because interfaces automatically save data to files
Because only one class can implement an interface
Because the interface forces both classes to expose the same `log` method
Because interfaces make both classes identical at runtime
It causes a syntax error
It returns `undefined` because `Dog` cannot be used as `Animal`
It returns `woof` because the overridden method in `Dog` is used
It returns `some sound` because the parameter type is `Animal`
